free_text: Isomeric cross sections and yield ratios of (d,p)
Metal foils were used as targets whenever possible.
If foils were unavailable, uniform (4%) layers of
powdered compounds were deposited on backings of
glass-fiber filter paper, weighed, covered with mylar
tape, and assembled into stacks with Al foils as beam
or with a calibrated 3 in. x 3 in. NaI(Tl) crystal and
were subjected to adaptations of standard
radiochemical procedures. The final samples were
mounted either on Al cards and covered with 0.9
mg/cm2 of mylar or between two films of aluminized
mylar on an aluminum ring. Absolute beta or gamma
counting was accomplished with proportional counter
